subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with clear purpose and focus
Research question is clearly stated, focused, and consistently connected to the discussion
Methodology is generally appropriate and clearly explained
weaknesses: Minor verbosity and occasional repetition slightly blur the sharpness of focus
Methodology lacks explicit justification for source selection and reflection on alternative approaches
Research question phrasing is verbose and includes dual aims, diffusing focus marginally
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs investigation demonstrates a strong and sustained focus. The topic is clearly defined and communicated, and the research question is visible and drives the analysis effectively. The methodology is appropriate, with a coherent plan for close readings, though the range of sources is narrow and lacks critical justification. Overall performance falls solidly in the top band, meriting 5 out of 6, with only minor issues around conciseness and methodological rationale preventing a perfect score.

strengths: Demonstrates solid knowledge of Whyteâs essays, with apt quotations and summaries
Accurate and mostly effective use of subject-specific terminology and concepts
Integration of intertextual references shows a coherent understanding of context
weaknesses: Reliance on journalistic secondary sources limits academic depth
Occasional imprecision and repetition in the use of terminology
Some informal phrasing interrupts the formal academic register
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay shows good knowledge and understanding of the primary texts and employs appropriate literary-critical concepts. Source usage is partially effective, reinforcing analysis without fully developing a nuanced scholarly conversation. Terminology is mostly accurate but not consistently sophisticated. These strengths align with a mid-band performance, justifying 4 out of 6.

strengths: Research is good and consistently relevant to the research question
Arguments are coherent and link close readings to the central claim about inner peace
Analysis includes perceptive discussion of literary devices such as juxtaposition
weaknesses: Scope of research is modest, with limited engagement with scholarly criticism or theory
Analysis often remains descriptive and occasionally repetitive
Critical evaluation of methodology, limitations, and implications is largely implicit
Conclusion omits reflection on study limitations or suggestions for further research
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates good critical thinking: research is focused and largely relevant, and the analysis moves beyond summary to build a coherent argument. However, it lacks depth in theoretical engagement and consistently critical evaluation, with several sections remaining descriptive. These characteristics place the investigation in the 7â9 band, and given the substantive analytical strengths offset by recurrent evaluative weaknesses, a score of 9 out of 12 accurately reflects the overall performance.

strengths: Clearly appropriate structure following IB EE requirements
Logical transitions and internal signposting aid reader navigation
Essential formatting elements (title, word count, table of contents, bibliography) are present and within limit
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation formatting and non-alphabetized bibliography entries
Overlong paragraphs and repetitive headings reduce readability
Instances of complex sentence structures obscure analysis
Repetition of the research question on each page interrupts flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is functional and meets formal requirements, with a clear, logical structure and correct inclusion of key elements. Yet inconsistencies in citation style, occasional layout lapses, and readability issues prevent a top-band performance. The overall quality corresponds to a mid-band score of 3 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The methodologyâclose reading of three essays supported by a handful of secondary web-based commentaries and interviewsâis **generally appropriate and explained** in the introduction. The student justifies the choice of the three words and outlines an analytical sequence. Yet the range of sources is narrow (largely popular articles rather than scholarly criticism), and there is little explicit reflection on alternative approaches or criteria for source selection. Thus the methodology is **mostly complete but not fully comprehensive or critically justified**.

Essential formatting elementsâtitle, word count, candidate code, consistent in-text citations, table of contents and bibliographyâare present, and the work appears within the 4000-word limit. Page references are listed in the contents, though actual pagination is not visible in the excerpt; citation style is broadly MLA but **inconsistently formatted and not alphabetised**, and some duplicated entries appear. These minor lapses reduce professionalism, yet layout remains **generally correct and functional**.

The investigation draws on the primary text in depth and integrates several secondary commentaries that are **largely relevant to the research question**. The selected evidence generally supports the discussion, and quotations are well chosen. Nevertheless, the research base is modestâlimited to a few online essays and interviewsâwith minimal engagement with scholarly criticism or linguistic theory, which restrains the overall breadth and authority. Hence the research is **good but not consistently excellent** in scope and relevance.

The student presents a **clear, focused topic**: an exploration of how David Whyteâs essays reinterpret everyday words to cultivate inner peace. The title is explicit and the opening paragraphs articulate the thematic scope, primary text and analytical angle, allowing the reader to understand immediately what the study will cover. Minor issues of verbosity and some repetition slightly blur the sharpness of focus, but overall the topic is **communicated accurately and purposefully**.

A coherent line of argument runs from introduction to conclusion, and the student **links individual chapter analyses to the overarching claim** that interpretation fosters inner peace. Some evaluative comments are present, particularly when weighing conventional versus Whyteâs readings. Yet evaluation of sources, implications, and limitations is **largely implicit and occasionally repetitive**, resulting in a discussion that is solid but not consistently critical or nuanced.

The analysis moves beyond summary, examining Whyteâs tonal choices, structure, and thematic links among essays. There are **clear efforts to interpret quotations and connect them to the argument about inner peace**. However, commentary often reiterates obvious points, and some claims remain **assertive rather than fully evidenced**; theoretical frameworks that could deepen insight (e.g., linguistics or literary theory) are absent. The depth is therefore good in parts but uneven, situating the work in the mid-band.

The essay follows the required IB EE structureâtitle page, abstract omitted per new guidelines, table of contents, introduction, body sections with clear headings, conclusion, and bibliography. Transitions between sections are logical, and internal signposting helps the reader navigate the argument. Minor issues arise from some **overlong paragraphs and repetitive headings**, but overall the structure is **clearly appropriate and fulfils formal requirements**.

Throughout the essay the student demonstrates **solid knowledge of Whyteâs text**, quoting aptly and summarising key ideas. References to secondary materials (Popova, Tippett) show awareness of broader discourse, though the selection is limited and largely journalistic rather than academic. Application of these sources is **partially effective**âthey are used to reinforce points rather than to develop a nuanced conversation. Overall, the student shows good but not outstanding command of content and context.

Key literary-critical terms such as **âtone,â ânarrative structure,â âmotif,â âintertextuality,â** and **âjuxtapositionâ** are employed accurately in most instances, indicating a reasonable grasp of subject-specific language. Some repetition and occasional imprecision (e.g., conflating thematic and stylistic concepts) prevent the usage from being consistently sophisticated, but the terminology is **mostly accurate and relevant** to the analysis presented.

The research question is **clearly stated verbatim** on the cover page and repeated throughout, ensuring sustained visibility. It is appropriately literary, manageable in scope (three selected essays) and consistently referenced in the body and conclusion. However, its wording is somewhat cumbersome and includes two aims (âprovide a new interpretationâ and âprove inner peaceâ), which marginally weakens focus. Nonetheless, the question remains sufficiently precise and drives the essayâs discussion effectively.

annotations.comment: Table of contents lists page numbers, yet the document pages are unnumbered. Numbering pages consistently will align the contents and improve navigation.

The introduction provides rich context but lacks an explicit thesis statement and a clear overview of the analytical criteria. A concise thesis outlining the central argument and method would strengthen focus.
The phrase âluring the reader into acceptingâ adopts a colloquial tone. Employ more formal academic language to maintain register consistency in literary analysis.
Effective use of literary-critical terminology in your heading and topic sentences demonstrates a solid grasp of subject-specific language.
Analysis of Whyteâs unpacking of âvulnerabilityâ is descriptive but would benefit from deeper evaluation of how these structural and tonal choices emotionally engage the reader.
The discussion of alternating positive and negative nuances effectively highlights Whyteâs use of juxtaposition to reshape the readerâs perception of vulnerability.
Repeating the research question on each page interrupts flow. It would improve readability to include it only on the cover page and once more in the introduction.

The research question drives the investigation but its phrasing is verbose and contains dual aims, which slightly diffuses focus. Consider streamlining it to a single, concise question to sharpen the essayâs purpose.
Word count and candidate code are correctly provided on the title page, meeting IB formatting requirements.

Methodology describes close reading and essay selection but does not justify why these three essays were chosen over others or reflect on alternative approaches. Adding this rationale would enhance methodological rigor.
The analysis of Ambition remains largely descriptive. Incorporating scholarly perspectives or theoretical frameworks could deepen the critique and support more nuanced conclusions.
Consider drawing on external definitions of âambitionâ from academic sources to contrast with Whyteâs interpretation and enrich your critical discussion.
Integration of intertextual references (Camus) demonstrates sophisticated understanding of how external texts can reinforce Whyteâs concept of courage.
The transition into the âCourageâ section lacks a clear signpost linking back to previous argument. An explicit topic sentence would strengthen structural coherence.
This section repeats descriptions of the essayâs content rather than critically evaluating Whyteâs rhetorical strategies. Focus on assessing the effectiveness of his techniques.
Your analysis of juxtaposition in the âCourageâ essay is perceptive, but you could further examine how shifting diction levels mirror the readerâs evolving engagement.
The conclusion effectively reiterates key points but omits reflection on study limitations or suggestions for further research. Including these would demonstrate deeper critical evaluation.
The bibliography is not alphabetized and contains duplicate entries of the same source. Reorder entries alphabetically and remove duplicates to comply with MLA guidelines.
Some paragraphs rely heavily on summary of the essay rather than critical evaluation. Strive to interrogate the implications of Whyteâs examples rather than reiterate them.
Sentences are excessively long and complex, which can obscure your analysis. Breaking them into shorter, focused statements will improve clarity and reader engagement.
Inâtext citations list only page numbers (e.g., â(8)â) without the authorâs name. To conform with MLA style, include both author and page number in parentheses.
Secondary sources are formatted inconsistently (punctuation and italics vary). Standardize each entry to a single style for professionalism and clarity.
